E0001.title: Invalid Character
E0001.help: Remove the illegal character or use a valid encoding
E0001.error_output:
error[E0001]: Invalid character
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = ¥100;
  |     ^ illegal character '¥'

E0002.title: Invalid Numeric Literal
E0002.help: Check the format of the numeric literal
E0002.error_output:
error[E0002]: Invalid numeric literal
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = 1_2_3_;
  |     ^ invalid numeric literal

E0003.title: Unterminated String
E0003.message: String literal is missing a closing quote
E0003.template: String starting at line {line} is unterminated
E0003.help: Add a closing quote to the string
E0003.error_output:
error[E0003]: Unterminated string
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = "hello world;
  |     ^ unterminated string

E0004.title: Invalid Character Literal
E0004.help: Character literals can only contain one character
E0004.error_output:
error[E0004]: Invalid character literal
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = 'ab';
  |     ^ Character literal contains multiple characters

E0010.title: Expected token
E0010.help: Check the syntax and add the expected token
E0010.error_output:
error[E0010]: Expected token
 --> example.yx:1:7
  |
1 | x = 1, 2;
  |       ^ Expected ',' or ';'

E0011.title: Unexpected token
E0011.help: Remove or replace the unexpected token
E0011.error_output:
error[E0011]: Unexpected token
 --> example.yx:1:7
  |
1 | x = 1 @ 2;
  |       ^ Unexpected token '@'

E0012.title: Invalid syntax
E0012.message: Syntax error in expression or statement
E0012.help: Check the syntax of the expression or statement
E0012.error_output:
error[E0012]: Invalid syntax
 --> example.yx:1:3
  |
1 | if x { }
  |    ^ Missing condition

E0013.title: Mismatched brackets
E0013.message: Parentheses, brackets, or braces are mismatched
E0013.template: Mismatched {bracket_type}: opened at line {open_line}, column {open_col}, not closed
E0013.help: Ensure all brackets are properly closed
E0013.error_output:
error[E0013]: Mismatched brackets
 --> example.yx:1:1
  |
1 | x = (1 + 2;
  |     ^ Unclosed parenthesis

E0014.title: Missing semicolon
E0014.help: Add a semicolon at the end of the statement
E0014.error_output:
error[E0014]: Missing semicolon
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = 1
  |       ^ Missing semicolon

E1001.title: Unknown variable
E1001.help: Check if the variable name is spelled correctly, or define it first
E1001.error_output:
error[E1001]: Unknown variable
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = y + 1;
  |       ^ Undefined variable 'y'

E1002.title: Type Mismatch
E1002.message: Expected type does not match actual type
E1002.help: Use the correct type or add a type cast
E1002.error_output:
error[E1002]: type mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:12
  |
1 | x: Int = "hello";
  |            ^ expected 'Int', found 'String'

E1003.title: Unknown Type
E1003.help: Check if the type name is spelled correctly
E1003.error_output:
error[E1003]: unknown type
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x: MyType = 1;
  |     ^ undefined type 'MyType'

E1010.title: Argument Count Mismatch
E1010.message: Function call arguments do not match the definition
E1010.help: Check the number of arguments in the function call
E1010.error_output:
error[E1010]: argument count mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:1
  |
1 | add(1, 2, 3);
  |     ^ function expected 2 arguments, found 3

E1011.title: Argument Type Mismatch
E1011.help: Ensure parameter types match the function signature
E1011.error_output:
error[E1011]: parameter type mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | add("hello", 2);
  |     ^ expected 'Int', found 'String'

E1012.title: Return type mismatch
E1012.help: Ensure the return value matches the declared return type
E1012.error_output:
error[E1012]: return type mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:19
  |
1 | fn foo() -> Int { "hello" }
  |                   ^ expected 'Int', found 'String'

E1013.title: Function not found
E1013.help: Check if the function name is spelled correctly
E1013.error_output:
error[E1013]: function not found
 --> example.yx:1:1
  |
1 | unknown_fn();
  | ^ undefined function 'unknown_fn'

E1020.title: Cannot infer type
E1020.message: Unable to infer type from context
E1020.help: Add type annotation or explicit type parameter
E1020.error_output:
error[E1020]: cannot infer type
 --> example.yx:1:9
  |
1 | let x = nil;
  |         ^ cannot infer type from 'nil'

E1021.title: Type inference conflict
E1021.message: Multiple constraints lead to type contradiction
E1021.error_output:
error[E1021]: Type inference conflict
 --> example.yx:1:11
  |
1 | let x: Int = "hello";
  |           ^ type annotation conflicts with inferred type

E1030.title: Incomplete pattern
E1030.message: match expression does not cover all cases
E1030.help: Add missing patterns to match expression
E1030.error_output:
error[E1030]: Incomplete pattern
 --> example.yx:1:1
  |
1 | match x { Some(_) => 1 }
  |     ^ pattern 'None' not covered

E1031.title: Unreachable pattern
E1031.help: Remove or modify the unreachable pattern
E1031.error_output:
error[E1031]: Unreachable pattern
 --> example.yx:1:18
  |
1 | match x { 1 => "one", 1 => "another" }
  |                  ^ pattern '1' is already covered

E1040.title: Operation not supported
E1040.message: Type does not support this operation
E1040.help: Check the operations supported by this type
E1040.error_output:
error[E1040]: Operation not supported
 --> example.yx:1:9
  |
1 | "hello" + 1;
  |         ^ String type does not support '+' operation

E1041.title: Index out of bounds
E1041.template: Index out of bounds: valid range is 0..{max}, found {index}
E1041.help: Ensure the index is within the collection bounds
E1041.error_output:
error[E1041]: Index out of bounds
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | arr[10];
  |     ^ Index 10 exceeds array length 5

E1042.title: Field not found
E1042.help: Check the available fields of the struct
E1042.error_output:
error[E1042]: Field not found
 --> example.yx:1:7
  |
1 | point.z;
  |       ^ Cannot find field 'z' in struct 'Point'

E1050.title: Boolean operand required
E1050.template: Logical operations require boolean operands, but found '{left}' and '{right}'
E1050.help: Convert the operands to boolean type or use other operators
E1050.error_output:
error[E1050]: boolean operands required
 --> example.yx:1:9
  |
1 | let x = 1 && 2;
  |         ^ logical operations require boolean operands

E1051.title: Logical NOT requires boolean operand
E1051.message: Logical NOT operation requires boolean operand
E1051.template: Logical NOT requires boolean operand, but found '{type}'
E1051.help: Convert the operand to boolean type before applying NOT
E1051.error_output:
error[E1051]: logical NOT requires boolean operand
 --> example.yx:1:9
  |
1 | let x = !42;
  |         ^ NOT requires boolean operand, but found 'Int'

E1052.title: Invalid dereference
E1052.template: Cannot dereference type '{type}', expected pointer type
E1052.help: Only pointer types can be dereferenced
E1052.error_output:
error[E1052]: invalid dereference
 --> example.yx:1:9
  |
1 | let x = *42;
  |         ^ cannot dereference 'Int'

E1053.title: Non-struct field access
E1053.message: Cannot access field on non-struct type
E1053.template: Cannot access field on non-struct type '{type}'
E1053.help: Field access only applies to struct types
E1053.error_output:
error[E1053]: non-struct field access
 --> example.yx:1:12
  |
1 | let x = 42.field;
  |            ^ cannot access field on 'Int'

E1054.title: conditional type mismatch
E1054.message: conditional expression must be boolean type
E1054.help: use a boolean expression or convert to boolean type
E1054.error_output:
error[E1054]: conditional type mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:4
  |
1 | if 42 { }
  |    ^ condition must be bool, found 'Int'

E1055.title: constraint in non-generic context
E1055.message: constraint type used in non-generic context
E1055.template: constraint type '{type}' can only be used in generic context
E1055.help: use concrete type instead of constraint type
E1055.error_output:
error[E1055]: constraint in non-generic context
 --> example.yx:1:8
  |
1 | let x: Eq = 42;
  |        ^ constraint type cannot be used here

E1060.title: type parameter count mismatch
E1060.message: incorrect number of type parameters provided
E1060.help: provide the correct number of type parameters
E1060.error_output:
error[E1060]: type parameter count mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:8
  |
1 | let x: Vec<Int, String> = vec![];
  |        ^ expected 1 type parameter, found 2

E1061.title: Cannot instantiate generic
E1061.message: Generic type cannot be instantiated with given arguments
E1061.template: Cannot instantiate generic type with given arguments
E1061.help: Ensure type parameters satisfy all constraints
E1061.error_output:
error[E1061]: cannot instantiate generic
 --> example.yx:1:9
  |
1 | let x = Foo::<Int>();
  |         ^ cannot instantiate with given arguments

E1070.title: Unknown label
E1070.help: Check the label name or define it on the loop
E1070.error_output:
error[E1070]: unknown label
 --> example.yx:1:7
  |
1 | break 'unknown;
  |       ^ unknown label 'unknown'

E1080.title: spawn can only be used within @block scope
E1080.help: Place `spawn { ... }` inside `@block { ... }`, or mark the outer function as `@block` (currently: @{mode}).

E1081.title: `?` can only be used within functions returning Result
E1081.help: Change the return type of the outer function to `Result[T, E]` first, then use `expr?` for error propagation.

E1082.title: `?` can only be used with Result expressions
E1082.help: Only use `?` on expressions of type `Result[T, E]`.

E1083.title: Error type mismatch for `?`
E1083.help: Ensure the error type `E` in the current function's return type `Result[_, E]` matches the error type of the unwrapped `Result[_, E]`.

E2001.title: Scope Error
E2001.message: Variable is not in the current scope
E2001.help: Check the variable's scope, ensure it is accessible here
E2001.error_output:
error[E2001]: scope error
 --> example.yx:1:8
  |
1 | fn foo() { x }
  |        ^ variable 'x' is not in scope

E2002.title: Duplicate Definition
E2002.message: Variable defined multiple times in the same scope
E2002.template: Duplicate definition: '{name}' is already defined in the current scope
E2002.help: Rename or remove the duplicate definition

error[E2002]: duplicate definition
 --> example.yx:1:11
  |
1 | let x = 1; let x = 2;
  |           ^ 'x' is already defined

error[E2003]: ownership error
 --> example.yx:1:19
  |
1 | let a = vec![1, 2, 3]; let b = a;
  |                   ^ value has been moved

E2010.title: Immutable Assignment
E2010.message: Attempted to modify an immutable variable

E2010.error_output:
error[E2010]: immutable assignment
 --> example.yx:1:11
  |
1 | let x = 1; x = 2;
  |           ^ cannot assign to immutable variable

E2011.title: Use of Uninitialized Variable
E2011.message: A variable was used without being initialized
E2011.error_output:
error[E2011]: use of uninitialized variable
 --> example.yx:1:12
  |
1 | let x; println!(x);
  |            ^ variable used without initialization

E2012.title: Mutability Conflict
E2012.message: Mutable reference used in an immutable context
E2012.template: Mutability conflict: cannot use mutable reference in an immutable context
E2012.help: Ensure the mutability of references matches the usage context
E2012.example: let mut v = vec![1, 2]; let r = &v; v.push(3);
E2012.error_output:
error[E2012]: mutability conflict
 --> example.yx:1:29
  |
1 | let mut v = vec![1, 2]; let r = &v; v.push(3);
  |                             ^ cannot modify through immutable reference

E2013.title: Variable Shadowing
E2013.help: Use a different variable name, or declare the variable in a different scope
E2013.error_output:
error[E2013]: variable shadowing
 --> example.yx:1:17
  |

  |                 ^ Cannot shadow existing variable 'i'

E2090.help: check if the function signature format is correct

1 | "(x: Unknown) -> Int"
  | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

  | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

E2094.help: Use different generic parameter names for inner function types

 --> std/list.yx:1:1

  | ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

E1090.title: ✨ Unspeakable ✨
E1090.message: You have reached the philosophical boundary of YaoXiang
E1090.help: This is the source of the language, the compiler falls silent here
E1090.zen_message:
╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                                                               ║
║   One produces two, two produces three, three produces all.   ║
║   The Yi has the Great Ultimate, which produces the two         ║
║   principles.                                                  ║
║                                                               ║
║   Type: Type = Type                                             ║
║   This is the source of the yao-xiang, the boundary of         ║
║   language.                                                    ║
║   The compiler falls silent here, philosophy dwells here.      ║
║                                                               ║
║   Thank you for reaching the philosophical boundary of         ║
║   language.                                                    ║
║                                                               ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

E2014.title: Use of moved value
E2014.template: '{name}' has been moved and cannot be used again
E2014.help: This value has been moved to a new owner. To use it again, use a reference or clone.

E2015.title: Borrowing after move
E2015.template: Cannot borrow '{name}' after it has been moved
E2015.help: The value has been moved. Use a reference before moving, or clone the value.

E2016.title: Immutable assignment
E2016.help: Declare the variable with `mut` to allow assignment.

E2017.title: Multiple mutable borrows
E2017.help: Ensure only one mutable reference exists at a time.

E2018.title: Mutable/immutable borrow conflict
E2018.template: Cannot mutably borrow '{name}' (it is already borrowed immutably)
E2018.help: Ensure that there are no immutable references before creating a mutable reference.

E2019.title: Double Free
E2019.help: Ensure that the value is freed only once.

E2020.title: Use After Free
E2020.help: Use the value before it is freed, or refactor the code.

E2021.title: Free of Moved Value
E2021.template: Cannot free '{name}': the value has been moved
E2021.help: The value has been moved and cannot be freed again.

E2022.title: Immutable Mutation
E2022.template: Cannot mutate immutable variable '{name}' via method '{method}'
E2022.help: Use `mut` to declare the variable to allow mutation.

E2023.title: Assignment to Immutable Field
E2023.template: Cannot assign to immutable field '{field}' of '{struct_name}'
E2023.help: Use `mut` to declare the field to allow assignment.

E2024.title: Reference to Non-Owner
E2024.template: Cannot create a reference to '{name}': the value is not owned
E2024.help: The value has been moved or freed; ensure the value is still owned.

E2025.title: Reassignment of Non-Mutable Variable
E2025.template: Cannot reassign '{name}': variable has not been moved yet
E2025.help: Only moved variables can be reassigned.

E2026.title: Consumed value not returned
E2026.template: Parameter '{name}' was consumed but not returned
E2026.help: Return the consumed value or use a reference.

E2027.title: Unsafe dereference
E2027.template: Cannot dereference raw pointer outside of unsafe block
E2027.help: Wrap the dereference operation in an `unsafe` block.

E2028.title: Clone moved value
E2028.template: Cannot clone '{name}': value has been moved
E2028.help: Value has been moved, clone before moving if multiple uses needed.

E3001.title: Unimplemented expression (IR)
E3001.help: This expression type is not yet supported in IR generation.

E3002.title: Unimplemented statement (IR)
E3002.help: This statement type is not yet supported in IR generation.

E3003.title: Invalid operand (IR)
E3003.help: Check the operand type and usage.

E3004.title: Unsupported Iterator
E3004.help: Currently only range and list iterators are supported.

E3005.title: IR Generation Error
E3005.help: This is an internal error. Please report this issue.

E3010.title: Unimplemented Expression (Code Generation)
E3010.help: This expression type is not yet supported in code generation.

E3011.title: Unimplemented Statement (Code Generation)
E3011.help: This statement type is not yet supported in code generation.

E3012.title: Unimplemented Call (Code Generation)
E3012.help: This call type is not yet supported in code generation.

E3013.title: Symbol Not Found (Code Generation)
E3013.help: Ensure the symbol is defined and accessible.

E3014.title: Register Overflow
E3014.help: Reduce the number of local variables or simplify the expression.

E3015.title: Invalid assignment target
E3015.help: Only variables and fields can be assigned to.

E3016.title: Type mismatch (code generation)
E3016.template: Code generation type mismatch: expected {expected}, found {found}
E3016.help: This is an internal error. Please report this issue.

E3017.title: Invalid operand (code generation)
E3017.help: Check the operand types and usage.

E3018.title: Translation error
E3018.help: This is an internal error. Please report this issue.

E4010.title: Division by zero in constant expression
E4010.template: Division by zero in constant expression
E4010.help: Ensure that the divisor in the constant expression is not zero.

E4011.title: Constant overflow
E4011.help: The result of the constant expression exceeds the range of the type.

E4012.title: Constant recursion too deep
E4012.template: Constant evaluation exceeds maximum recursion depth {limit}
E4012.help: Simplify constant expressions to reduce recursion.

E4013.title: Non-constant function
E4013.template: Non-constant function '{func}' used in constant context
E4013.help: Only functions marked as constant can be used in constant expressions.

E4014.title: Constant evaluation failed
E4014.help: Ensure the expression can be evaluated at compile time.

E4015.title: Loop cannot be proven to terminate
E4015.template: Loop cannot be proven to terminate: no strictly decreasing measure detected in loop body
E4015.help: Ensure loop variables change monotonically towards the termination condition, e.g. while i < n { i += 1 }.

E4016.title: Recursion cannot be proven to terminate
E4016.template: Recursive call cannot be proven to terminate: arguments of function '{func}' do not strictly decrease in recursive call
E4016.help: Ensure recursive calls use smaller arguments, e.g. factorial(n-1).

E4017.title: Measure not strictly decreasing
E4017.template: Measure '{measure}' not strictly decreasing on all loop paths
E4017.help: Ensure variables change towards the termination condition in all branches of the loop body.

E4018.title: Refinement predicate violation
E4018.help: The predicate has been falsified — there exists an assignment that makes the constraint false. Check variable values or relax the constraint.

E4019.title: Type equality does not hold
E4019.message: Type equality falsified within the proof pipeline
E4019.template: Type equality does not hold: expected {expected}, found {found}
E4019.help: Check the type computation of the component or provide a proof function.

E5006.title: Duplicate import
E5006.template: Duplicate import: '{name}' has already been imported

E5007.title: Module export
E5007.help: Available exports are listed in the error message

E6006.title: Function not found (runtime)
E6006.help: Ensure the function is defined and spelled correctly

E6007.title: Runtime error
E6007.help: See the error message for details

E8004.title: Unimplemented Feature
E8004.help: This feature is not yet implemented.

E0015.title: Expected Identifier
E0015.message: Parser expected an identifier or name
E0015.help: Provide a valid identifier at this position
E0015.error_output:
error[E0015]: Expected identifier
 --> example.yx:1:4
  |
1 | fn () {}
  |    ^ Expected function name

E0016.title: Expected Expression
E0016.help: Provide a valid expression at this position
E0016.error_output:
error[E0016]: Expected expression
 --> example.yx:1:5
  |
1 | x = ;
  |     ^ Expected expression after '='

E0017.title: Deprecated Syntax
E0017.message: Old syntax is no longer supported
E0017.template: Old syntax '{old}' is no longer supported. Please use '{new}'
E0017.help: Use the new syntax as suggested
E0017.error_output:
error[E0017]: Old syntax is deprecated
 --> example.yx:1:1
  |
1 | Type[T]
  | ^^^^ Old syntax; please use 'Type' alone

E0018.title: Keyword as name
E0018.message: Keywords cannot be used as variable or expression names
E0018.template: '{keyword}' is a keyword and cannot be used as a name
E0018.help: Use a different identifier instead of the keyword
E0018.error_output:
error[E0018]: Keyword as name
 --> example.yx:1:1
  |
1 | if = 1;
  | ^^ 'if' is a keyword

E0019.title: Argument count mismatch
E0019.help: Ensure the argument count matches the signature
E0019.example: f: (a: Int) -> Int = (a, b) => a + b
E0019.error_output:
error[E0019]: Argument count mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:20
  |
1 | f: (a: Int) -> Int = (a, b) => a + b
  |                    ^ expected 1 argument, found 2

E0020.title: Parameter name mismatch
E0020.message: Parameter name does not match signature
E0020.template: Parameter name mismatch (at position {position}): expected '{expected}', found '{found}'
E0020.help: Use matching parameter names, or omit the lambda header
E0020.error_output:
error[E0020]: Parameter name mismatch
 --> example.yx:1:20
  |
1 | f: (a: Int) -> Int = (b) => b
  |                    ^ expected 'a', found 'b'

E0021.title: Expected token
E0021.message: Parser expected a specific token after a specific context
E0021.help: Add the expected token at the correct position
E0021.error_output:
error[E0021]: Expected token
 --> example.yx:1:7
  |
1 | mut x Int
  |       ^ Expected ':' after 'mut'

E0022.title: Enums must use brace syntax
E0022.message: Enum variants must use brace syntax
E0022.template: Enum variants must use brace syntax: {| variant1 | variant2 | ... }
E0022.help: Wrap enum variants with braces and pipe separators
E0022.error_output:
error[E0022]: Enums must use brace syntax
 --> example.yx:1:9
  |
1 | Color = red | green | blue
  |         ^ Use brace syntax: { red | green | blue }
